title: DevOps Learning Path
description: Unified roadmap from software delivery fundamentals to elite production SRE and platform engineering readiness.
visual_hint: DevOps infinity loop with glowing integration and feedback nodes
trainer_notes: Objectives: Understand the DevOps lifecycle. Recommended Order: Foundations -> Git -> Automation -> CI/CD -> Containers -> IaC -> Orchestration -> Cloud -> Networking -> Security -> Observability -> Testing -> Culture. Est: 90 Days.
cto_notes: Business Value: Shrink lead time to production, increase deployment frequency, reduce change failure rates, and boost customer satisfaction KPIs. Risk/Benefit: High initial training cost vs. massive long-term operations automation dividend.
engineer_notes: Real-world Workflow: Local code change -> Git pull request -> CI pipeline (Lint/Build/Test) -> Artifact build -> GitOps Deployment -> Monitoring/Logs telemetry loop.
exercise: Review the entire roadmap topology and map it to your personal career learning target.

children.title: Foundations & OS
children.description: Operating system internals, Linux kernel features, process management, and storage mechanics required to run production services.
children.visual_hint: Tux the Linux Penguin icon, terminal prompt window
children.trainer_notes: Objectives: Master Linux command line, understand processes, signals, and filesystems. Order: 1st node. Est: 7 days. Tips: Run command line drills, host a terminal simulator lab, and quiz on file descriptors.
children.cto_notes: Business Value: Reduced infrastructure license costs (Open Source Linux) and stable foundation for running containerized workloads. Risk: High reliance on system administration knowledge. KPIs: Uptime, OS patching frequency, resource utilization.
children.engineer_notes: Workflow: Checking system health, disk space, and process tree. Common Errors: 'Out of memory (OOM)' kills -> adjust swap or limits. Snippet: `ulimit -n 65536` for file descriptor limits.
children.exercise: Deploy a custom systemd service that runs a lightweight Python web server on boot, and configure logrotate to manage its logs.

children.title: Version Control (Git)
children.description: Decentralized version control workflows, branching strategies, and repository collaboration models.
children.visual_hint: Branching Git tree diagram showing merges and pull requests
children.trainer_notes: Objectives: Learn version control commands, conflict resolution, and trunk-based development vs GitFlow. Est: 5 days. Tips: Practice merge conflict workshops, run simulated team pull requests.
children.cto_notes: Business Value: Frictionless developer collaboration, auditable change log history for compliance, and automated trigger points for release pipelines. KPIs: PR lifecycle lead time, pull request approval rate.
children.engineer_notes: Workflow: Feature branch branching from main, commit, rebase on main, push to origin, raise PR. Fix: If you commit to the wrong branch, run `git reset --soft HEAD~1`.
children.exercise: Create two local directories, simulate a concurrent change on the same line of a text file, commit, and manually resolve the resulting merge conflict.

children.title: Continuous Integration & Deployment (CI/CD)
children.description: Automated pipelines that run linting, tests, build artifacts, and orchestrate deployments upon repository commits.
children.visual_hint: Pipeline gears or automated assembly line showing Git trigger -> Build -> Test -> Deploy
children.trainer_notes: Objectives: Understand triggers, environment variables, caching, and building deployment stages. Est: 10 days. Tips: Build a pipeline from scratch using GitHub Actions, configure matrix builds.
children.cto_notes: Business Value: Instant developer feedback loop, zero-downtime automated deployments, and eliminated manual deployment errors. KPIs: Change Failure Rate, Deployment Frequency, Build Duration.
children.engineer_notes: Workflow: Push triggers workflow -> Node/Go build -> run unit tests -> docker build -> ECR push -> ECS deploy. Common issue: Caching node_modules saves 50% build time.
children.exercise: Write a `.github/workflows/ci.yml` pipeline that triggers on pull requests, runs linting/formatting tests, builds a Docker image, and fails if the test coverage drops below 80%.

children.title: Containers (Docker)
children.description: Application packaging, image layered filesystems, isolation mechanics, and local developer environment virtualization.
children.visual_hint: Docker blue shipping container whale icon
children.trainer_notes: Objectives: Learn namespaces/cgroups isolation, write Dockerfiles, manage layers, cache optimization. Est: 7 days. Tips: Compare standard builds with multi-stage builds side-by-side to show size difference.
children.cto_notes: Business Value: Standardized execution environment eliminating the 'works on my machine' bug, fast container cold start, and higher compute density. KPIs: Image size (MB), vulnerability scans count, container boot time.
children.engineer_notes: Workflow: Run app locally in container, build multi-stage production image, push to registry. Common issue: Image is too large (1GB+) -> use alpine/distroless bases and place static assets in early stages.
children.exercise: Containerize a Node.js API using a multi-stage Dockerfile that splits dev dependencies from production dependencies, reducing final image size below 100MB.

children.title: Orchestration (Kubernetes)
children.description: Clustered container execution, self-healing architecture, declarative desired-state scaling, and cloud-native application management.
children.trainer_notes: Objectives: Understand control plane vs node, pods, services, ingress, configuration management, statefulsets. Est: 14 days. Tips: Use Minikube/Kind for hands-on, simulate pod deletions to show self-healing.
children.cto_notes: Business Value: Auto-scaling infrastructure to match traffic demand, cost-efficient resource scheduling, and high availability across nodes. KPIs: Node scheduling latency, HPA scaling speed, Pod crash loop rates.
children.engineer_notes: Workflow: Deploy YAML manifests -> expose via service -> define Ingress. Common issue: 'CrashLoopBackOff' -> run `kubectl logs` and `kubectl describe pod` to diagnose resource starvation or start script failures.
children.exercise: Deploy a multi-tier guestbook app (React front-end, Redis back-end) in Kind, configure secrets for database connection strings, and scale pods dynamically using Horizontal Pod Autoscaler (HPA).

children.title: Infrastructure as Code (IaC)
children.description: Declarative provisioning and lifecycle management of cloud resources via version-controlled code files.
children.visual_hint: Terraform logo or code blueprint representing cloud resources like VMs, databases, and networks
children.trainer_notes: Objectives: Understand immutable infrastructure, Terraform state files, modules, providers, plans. Est: 10 days. Tips: Emphasize 'always run terraform plan' and state lock mechanics using AWS S3 + DynamoDB.
children.cto_notes: Business Value: Repeatable environment provisioning, disaster recovery speed, cloud budget visibility, and automated infrastructure audit logs. KPIs: Time to provision new staging env, configuration drift counts.
children.engineer_notes: Workflow: Write HCL configs -> terraform plan -> review changes -> terraform apply. Fix: State lock error -> inspect lock ID in DynamoDB or release manually if safe.
children.exercise: Create a reusable Terraform module to launch an AWS VPC with private and public subnets, an Internet Gateway, and a NAT Gateway, keeping the state locked in S3.

children.title: Cloud Platforms (AWS/GCP/Azure)
children.description: Elastic cloud resource delivery including virtual computing instances, managed databases, load balancing, and identity access control.
children.visual_hint: Cloud drawing with logos of AWS, GCP, and Azure inside
children.trainer_notes: Objectives: Understand IAM policies, security groups, public/private subnets, block storage, and serverless compute. Est: 10 days. Tips: Focus heavily on IAM least privilege rules, set up billing alerts immediately.
children.cto_notes: Business Value: Zero hardware procurement lead time, pay-as-you-go elastic billing, and global market reach. Risk: Cloud sprawl and runaway costs. KPIs: Cloud spend budget compliance, AWS Security Hub scoring.
children.engineer_notes: Workflow: Provision EC2, configure IAM Role, attach Security Group allowing port 443 only. Fix: Instance can't talk to internet -> verify Route Table associates public subnet with Internet Gateway.
children.exercise: Configure an AWS S3 bucket to serve a static website securely, restricting access using S3 Bucket Policies and enabling CloudFront CDN with SSL.

children.title: Observability & Monitoring
children.description: Telemetry collection, structured logging, transaction tracing, and dashboard visualization to maintain system reliability.
children.visual_hint: Line graph dashboard showing metrics going up and down, magnifying glass examining log files
children.trainer_notes: Objectives: Differentiate between Metrics (numbers), Logs (events), and Traces (journey). Master Prometheus Query Language (PromQL) and Grafana. Est: 8 days. Tips: Set up an alertmanager rule to trigger a webhook.
children.cto_notes: Business Value: Pre-emptive failure detection, reduced Mean Time to Detect (MTTD), and business transaction intelligence. KPIs: MTTD, MTTR, user session error rate, query latency.
children.engineer_notes: Workflow: Prometheus scrapes `/metrics` endpoint -> displays in Grafana dashboard -> Alertmanager notifications to Slack on high CPU. Fix: Missing metrics -> check scraper target endpoints.
children.exercise: Deploy Prometheus and Grafana in Docker Compose. Configure Node Exporter to export host metrics, scrape them in Prometheus, and build a Grafana dashboard visualizing CPU, Memory, and Disk IO.

children.title: Security & Compliance (DevSecOps)
children.description: Integrating security checks into code repositories, build systems, container runtime environments, and automated secret management.
children.visual_hint: Shield with a keyhole, pipeline checkmark with safety lock
children.trainer_notes: Objectives: Learn secret scanning, static analysis (SAST), software composition analysis (SCA), and runtime protection. Est: 7 days. Tips: Do a demo showing Gitguardian/Trivy catching a dummy secret key.
children.cto_notes: Business Value: Avoid data leaks, secure user trust, ensure SOC2/HIPAA compliance, and prevent costly security breaches. KPIs: Unresolved vulnerabilities count, secret exposure count, time-to-patch CVEs.
children.engineer_notes: Workflow: Commit check (Trivy scans Dockerfile) -> Build check (SonarQube reviews code) -> Deploy check (Vault inserts DB credentials at runtime). Fix: Secret leaked -> instantly revoke the key and roll credentials.
children.exercise: Write a script that runs a Trivy scan on your Docker build artifact before pushing to your registry, failing the pipeline if a 'High' or 'Critical' vulnerability is discovered.

children.title: Networking & Storage
children.description: Network routing, DNS resolution, traffic load balancing, and persistent persistent volumes for stateful applications.
children.visual_hint: Router network layout showing clients, DNS, load balancers, and target pods
children.trainer_notes: Objectives: Master TCP/IP basics, DNS records (A, CNAME, TXT), HTTP/HTTPS protocols, SSL/TLS handshakes, load balancing, block vs object storage. Est: 7 days. Tips: Conduct DNS lookup and traceroute labs.
children.cto_notes: Business Value: Fast content delivery speed, secured data-at-rest encryption compliance, and resilient network traffic failovers. KPIs: DNS lookup speed, SSL certificate renewal success rate, latency (ms).
children.engineer_notes: Workflow: User visits website -> DNS points to ALB -> SSL terminated -> requests routed to Kubernetes Node Port. Fix: DNS propagation takes time -> use low TTL values for critical switchovers.
children.exercise: Generate a Let's Encrypt SSL certificate using Certbot, configure Nginx to terminate SSL traffic, and proxy pass requests to an internal application container.

children.title: Testing & Release Management
children.description: Zero-downtime deployment pipelines, safety testing in staging environments, and gradual progressive delivery of new application features.
children.visual_hint: A/B split path, traffic controller directing code deployments to different user subsets
children.trainer_notes: Objectives: Differentiate Blue-Green deployments (instant switch) from Canary releases (incremental traffic routing) and understand feature flags. Est: 5 days. Tips: Use Argo Rollouts in a demo.
children.cto_notes: Business Value: Safe software releases with minimal customer blast radius if errors occur, instant rollback capability, and market feedback via user subset trials. KPIs: Deployment rollback count, change failure recovery speed.
children.engineer_notes: Workflow: Deploy v2 version -> route 10% traffic using ALB/Ingress -> monitor error rates -> promote to 100% or rollback if logs show 500 errors. Fix: Route 100% traffic back to v1 instantly.
children.exercise: Set up a Canary deployment using Argo Rollouts or a custom Nginx routing file where 10% of HTTP header requests for a specific user group are sent to the v2 backend version.

children.title: Automation & Scripting
children.description: Scripting recurring operational tasks, parsing configurations, and scheduling pipeline workflows.
children.visual_hint: BASH prompt code scroll, cron clock icon
children.trainer_notes: Objectives: Master Bash script loops, inputs, variables, error handling, python script orchestration, and cron schedules. Est: 7 days. Tips: Review shell scripts for best practice linting (using Shellcheck).
children.cto_notes: Business Value: Eliminated manual labor costs, fast configuration runs, and standardized server setup processes. KPIs: Task execution speed, cron failure rates, automation coverage.
children.engineer_notes: Workflow: Cron triggers bash script -> runs database dump -> copies to S3 bucket. Fix: Always use absolute paths in cron jobs since cron doesn't load standard user environment variables.
children.exercise: Write a bash script that parses a log file using awk/sed, identifies IP addresses with more than 100 bad requests, and automatically appends them to a blocklist file.

children.title: Culture & Processes (SRE)
children.description: DevOps organizational patterns, service level budgeting, blameless reviews, and collaborative team communication systems.
children.visual_hint: Puzzle pieces fitting together, target board showing SLO/SLA lines
children.trainer_notes: Objectives: Understand the CAMS model (Culture, Automation, Measurement, Sharing). Differentiate SLA, SLO, and SLI. Write blameless post-mortems. Est: 5 days. Tips: Host a simulated post-mortem review.
children.cto_notes: Business Value: High-morale engineering culture, collaborative blameless environment, data-driven feature priority planning using Error Budgets. KPIs: Error Budget burn rate, user satisfaction (SLA compliance).
children.engineer_notes: Workflow: Monitor system latency (SLI) -> maintain target below 200ms (SLO) -> if target is breached, pause deployment feature releases and focus on reliability tasks. Fix: Run post-mortem to prevent recurrences.
children.exercise: Write a blameless post-mortem for a simulated 1-hour database outage, describing the timeline, root cause (e.g. disk exhaustion), immediate mitigations, and preventive action items.
